hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in TM5/ECL2, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity in the SSTR2-specific ECL3 pocket, thereby increasing selectivity margin over off-target subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that introducing mutations at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) will improve selectivity by reducing off-target interactions while preserving SSTR2 binding. This hypothesis is supported by the observed Δmargin trends and the need to disrupt conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing design on these positions will allow us to disrupt off-target contacts while preserving the critical FWKT(7-10) motif and Cys3/Cys14.

Introducing charged or small residues at positions 1 and 2 can disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Replacing residues at positions 5, 6, 11, and 12 with smaller residues can reduce off-target hydrophobic interactions.
